mechanism: Artemisinin contains an endoperoxide bridge that generates reactive intermediates in heme-rich parasites, producing rapid antimalarial activity.
safetyNotes: Standardized antimalarial regimens are generally tolerated, but unsupervised nonstandard products may lead to underdosing or toxicity. Use caution in pregnancy unless regimen is clinician-guided.

sources.title: WHO Guidelines for malaria (artemisinin-based therapy)

sideEffects: May cause allergic reactions or nausea
